Canada launches consultations on oil and gas emissions cap, Alberta warns against federal interference   The federal government has launched consultations on a plan to cap and cut greenhouse gases from the oil and gas sector but the Alberta government is already pushing back, arguing Ottawa cannot act unilaterally to meet emissions targets.

David Parker: Around Town with Wood Automotive Group's new Volkswagen dealership in Okotoks   The Wood Automotive Group has opened its newest dealership, Okotoks Volkswagen, in the heart of the community on Westland Road. It is the second company dealership serving the town and surrounding area, joining Okotoks Ford Lincoln, which has been a fixture in the Foothills area for more than 30 years.

Your letters for July 19, 2022   Stampede chastised over “Seniors’ Day” Standing in a crowd of seniors on Tuesday, July 12, waiting for the Stampede gates to open, I heard complaints floating around me. It seems that “Senior’s Day” was not for seniors this year. Instead, they decided to let everyone in on Community Day. What about a staggered entry?

Breakenridge: Posturing over a provincial sales tax doesn't solve Alberta's revenue issues   Being in favour of a provincial sales tax is dangerous politics at the best of times in Alberta, let alone during a race to lead the governing UCP. There was certainly no drama in the lead-up to this race as to whether any candidate might boldly declare their support for the idea.

Morton: Stars are aligning for a better deal for Alberta and all provinces   According to Calgary Herald columnist Don Braid’s account of last weekend’s federal Conservative Stampede Barbecue, the divisions within the Conservative Party of Canada were “on display.” Maybe Don should have listened a little more closely. And certainly, the jerk who booed Jean Charest should have. Because if you did listen carefully, you saw a window of opportunity — not just for the CPC, but for all of Canada — to defeat the Liberals’ “one-size-fits-all” approach to governing Canada and to rediscover the older and truer meaning of Canada’s diversity: federalism.

Braid: Aheer says race may be over unless moderates buy UCP memberships   Leela Aheer is the loud and proud progressive in the UCP leadership race.

True Crime Byline: How one childhood incident pushed Dawn Crey into a downward spiral from which she would never escape   The new podcast True Crime Byline looks back at The Vancouver Sun investigation into Vancouver's missing women, a case that eventually led to the arrest and conviction of Robert Pickton. This profile of missing woman Dawn Crey was part of a series of investigative stories published in 2001.

Jennifer Lopez, Ben Affleck obtain wedding license in Nevada   NEW YORK (AP) — Jennifer Lopez and Ben Affleck were wed Saturday in a late-night Las Vegas drive-through chapel, culminating a relationship that stretched over two decades in two separate romances and headlined countless tabloid covers.

Brooks: Nut Ale and DLA Piper celebrate Stampede   Getting back in the saddle never felt so good. Dozens of events supporting myriad causes enveloped our fair city during Stampede week and Calgarians came out en masse to support events after a seemingly endless hiatus.

Brooks: U.S. Consul Independence Day celebration a welcome return   Scores of business leaders, politicians, academics and more gathered on July 7 at the residence of the U.S. Consul to celebrate the 246th anniversary of the Independence of the United States of America. Pandemic restrictions nixed the celebration last year, so it was a welcome opportunity to socially gather at long last and show support for our neighbours.

Dean Goodine's new memoir chronicles a life in the Alberta and British Columbia film industries   The cover of his memoir They Don’t Pay Me To Say No: My Life in Film and Television Props is a picture of prop master Dean Goodine on the Longview, Alta., set of the 2000 TV film The Virginian with Diane Lane and Bill Pullman. He has what appears to be a stiff, full-grown cow slung over his shoulder. It was not a real corpse, it just played one on TV. It was for a scene when Lane’s school teacher character arrives in the rugged west to begin a new life and sees a cow lying dead in the grass.
Aribim Pepple leaving Cavalry at the transfer window   ATCO Field is home sweet home for Cavalry FC these days.

Roughriders announce 'several' positive COVID tests following Touchdown Atlantic game   REGINA — The Saskatchewan Roughriders say “several members” of the team have recently tested positive for COVID-19.

Ambrosie doesn't feel chippy game negatively impacted Touchdown Atlantic experience   TORONTO — CFL commissioner Randy Ambrosie doesn’t believe a chippy game between the Toronto Argonauts and Saskatchewan Roughriders negatively impacted the 2022 Touchdown Atlantic experience.

Bull riders battle to share Calgary Stampede title; Family affair in saddle bronc finale   Dakota Buttar didn’t mind sharing the spotlight with Shane Proctor at the Calgary Stampede rodeo on  Sunday .

Kris Molle wheels to first title at Calgary Stampede's Rangeland Derby   Kris Molle broke onto the scene at the Calgary Stampede when he won the Orville Strandquist Memorial Award as the top rookie chuckwagon driver in 2014.

Penner: Find your (mostly) peace and quiet on Alberta's backroads   Just before 5 a.m., a rooster crowed, the people camping beside me finally shut it down for the night, and an angry mess of black clouds started swirling high above me in the pink-grey dawn. Time to rise and shine. The Rowley light show was about to begin. And my boogie down the backroads was nearing its dramatic climax.

Last: Exploring the wine road less travelled   Wine, like many other pursuits, can slip into the mundane on occasion but you can remedy that by expanding your viniferous horizons and delving into the unknown. It’s estimated that as many as 10,000 vinifera varieties are growing on the planet, so it would figure that there are options to suit every whim and culinary pursuit. Got the chardonnay blahs? How about a crisp glass of pecorino or trebbiano spoletino (both from Italy)? Tired of getting slapped in the palate with cabernet and shiraz? Perhaps a Spanish mencia will enjoy meeting some flank steak and grilled peppers. Greece produces some excellent wines from grapes I still can’t pronounce but that won’t stop me from drinking them. The bottom line is that there are very few countries on earth that don’t grow grapes and make wine. It might come as a surprise to some that China is now the largest grape-growing country in the world and, in fairness, some of them are very good (I would like to think that the ethics of grape growing in China are better than other factions of Chinese industry, but I don’t know this to be factual). Let’s go exploring.

You have better odds of being struck by lightening than getting myocarditis after a COVID-19 vaccine   But if you land in hospital because of the virus, the odds of developing heart inflammation —and risking serious heart damage and possibly death — goes up more than thirty times.

What it feels like: Pelvic exenteration for colorectal cancer   After battling colorectal cancer and having his descending colon, rectum, prostate and bladder removed, Timothy Sproule is back to work full time and says his experiences have only made him a better surgeon.
